af_score_components.notes: Paper search MCP with OpenAI integration. Has MCP (quality 70). Searches academic papers via OpenAI. Documentation moderate. OpenAI API key required. Rate limits from OpenAI. Research automation focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (OpenAI). OpenAI API key required. Queries sent to OpenAI (privacy concern for sensitive research). Industry-standard API security. Credential management standard. Research metadata public but query patterns reveal interests.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on OpenAI API stability (enterprise SLA). Paper database stability varies. Rate limits managed by OpenAI. No SLA for MCP wrapper. Suitable for research automation with resilience to API rate limits.

af_score_components.notes: Adaptive Graph of Thoughts MCP for reasoning. Has MCP (quality 75). LLM reasoning framework. No auth required (stateless). Documentation adequate for ML context. Rate limits depend on LLM backend.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS for network calls. No auth for local reasoning. LLM backend auth depends on implementation. Graph reasoning outputs depend on input data security. No PII guarantees (depends on what agent passes in).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Reliability depends on LLM backend. Graph reasoning is stateless. No SLA. Suitable for experimental reasoning workflows. Breaking changes likely as reasoning paradigms evolve.

af_score_components.notes: adbfriend — likely an ADB (Android Debug Bridge) tool. No MCP documented. Minimal documentation. Command-line tool for Android development. No typical API.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool for device communication. ADB has inherent security risks if device is compromised. No network encryption (local USB/network). Device access requires trust.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on Android SDK and ADB stability. Device connection reliability varies. No SLA. Community tool.

af_score_components.notes: Storybook MCP addon (storybookjs/addon-mcp). Has MCP (quality 75). Official Storybook integration. Excellent documentation. No auth required (local tool). Rate limits not applicable (local).

security_score_components.security_notes: Local development tool (no network security critical). No auth required. Access to project files (developer responsibility). Open-source Storybook (good dependency hygiene). Development environment trust model.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Official Storybook project. Active development. Semantic versioning. No SLA (dev tool). Local reliability (developer environment-dependent). Suitable for component documentation automation.

af_score_components.notes: CLIO Adios MCP. Has MCP (quality 60). Legal practice management integration. Documentation limited. Legal software typically requires authentication. Rate limits unclear. Specialized use case (legal ops).

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Authentication for legal software (strong auth model). Legal data (privileged communication, client info) requires protection. Likely SOC2 compliance for legal practice. Fine-grained access controls needed.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Legal software vendors typically have good reliability. CLIO is established vendor. Professional SLA expected. Integration complexity varies.

af_score_components.notes: ADLS (Azure Data Lake Storage) MCP server by erikhoward. Has MCP (quality 75). Cloud storage integration. Documentation adequate. Azure credentials required. Rate limits from Azure. Data engineering focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Azure). Azure authentication (strong OAuth/token model). RBAC for storage access. SOC2/ISO compliant infrastructure. Credential management via Azure SDK. Excellent security posture for cloud storage.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Azure ADLS has enterprise SLA and status page. Official Microsoft infrastructure. High availability and disaster recovery. Retry logic documented. Suitable for data engineering pipelines with strong reliability.

af_score_components.notes: admin-mcp — generic admin tool. Has MCP (quality 60). Documentation minimal. Purpose unclear from name. Likely framework or admin panel. Auth model uncertain.

security_score_components.security_notes: Admin tools require careful security. Auth likely required. Scope control essential (prevents unauthorized admin actions). Documentation insufficient to assess.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Insufficient information. Admin tools typically need high reliability. No documentation implies limited maturity.

af_score_components.notes: ads — extremely minimal information. Could be advertising, data structures, or something else. No MCP documented. Name too generic for evaluation.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cannot evaluate without understanding purpose. Assume generic web security baseline.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Cannot evaluate. Needs clarification on what 'ads' actually is.

af_score_components.notes: Advanced Home Assistant MCP. Has MCP (quality 80). Smart home automation. Documentation good. Home Assistant auth (token-based). Rate limits from Home Assistant. IoT automation focused.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Home Assistant token-based auth (strong). Granular automations/entity access. Home Assistant infrastructure (active security focus). Credential management standard. IoT security considerations (device network access).

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Home Assistant is mature open-source project. Active development with good semver. No SLA (open-source) but community support strong. Suitable for smart home automation with high reliability.

af_score_components.notes: Advanced PocketBase MCP server. Has MCP (quality 75). Backend-as-a-service integration. Documentation adequate. PocketBase auth required. Rate limits documented. Suitable for database-driven agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. PocketBase authentication (strong OAuth/token model). Fine-grained permissions via collections and rules. Row-level security available. Excellent security posture for BaaS. Open-source allows audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: PocketBase is production-ready backend. Semantic versioning. Active development. No SLA for self-hosted (customer responsibility). Suitable for data-driven applications with good reliability.
